description: Multiple choice and scenario-based problems for AI Literacy domain

problems.problem_statement: What is the primary difference between traditional programming and machine learning?

choices.text: Machine learning requires more memory than traditional programming
Traditional programming uses explicit rules, while machine learning learns patterns from data
Machine learning is faster than traditional programming
Traditional programming cannot process large datasets

problems.problem_statement: Which of the following is an example of supervised learning?

choices.text: A system that clusters customers based on purchasing behavior without labels
A spam filter trained on emails labeled as 'spam' or 'not spam'
A robot learning to walk through trial and error
A game AI that discovers new strategies by playing itself
When a model performs well on training data but poorly on new data
When a model requires too much computational power
When training takes too long to complete
When the dataset is too large for the model

problems.problem_statement: Which AI ethics concern is most relevant when a hiring algorithm systematically rejects candidates from certain demographics?

choices.text: Privacy violation
Algorithmic bias and discrimination
Job displacement
Environmental impact
Designing better computer hardware for AI systems
Crafting effective inputs to get desired outputs from language models
Writing code to train neural networks
Debugging AI algorithms

problems.problem_statement: Which scenario represents a potential misuse of facial recognition technology?

choices.text: Using it to unlock your personal smartphone
Mass surveillance of public spaces without consent
Identifying your own photos on your device
Tagging friends in social media photos with their permission

problems.problem_statement: What is the 'training data' in machine learning?

choices.text: The hardware used to train the model
The data used to teach the model patterns and relationships
The final test given to developers
Documentation for the AI system

problems.problem_statement: Why is data privacy important when training AI systems?

choices.text: Personal data could be exposed or used inappropriately
It makes the AI run faster
Privacy is not important for AI systems
Only to comply with legal requirements
Moving training data from one server to another
Using knowledge from one task to improve learning on a related task
Training multiple models simultaneously
Converting a model from one programming language to another

problems.problem_statement: In the context of Large Language Models (LLMs), what is a 'hallucination'?

choices.text: When the model generates plausible-sounding but false information
When the model produces too much output
When the model refuses to answer a question
When the training process fails

problems.problem_statement: Match each AI concept with its correct description.

pairs.description: Computing system inspired by biological neurons
Examples used to teach a model
Using a trained model to make predictions

problems.problem_statement: Put the following steps of the machine learning workflow in the correct order:
